data.title: Walker & Dunlop Arranges $237.5 Million Refinancing for New York Community
data.description:
Walker & Dunlop said Tuesday it arranged $237.5 million in financing for the Edge-on-Hudson mixed-use community and The Daymark condominium, located within Edge-on-Hudson, in Sleepy Hollow, New York.

The funding was provided by Hudson Bay Capital. The Walker & Dunlop New York Capital Markets team arranged the refinancing package as exclusive advisers to King Street Capital Management and Biddle Real Estate Ventures, according to the announcement.

Shares of Walker & Dunlop were up 1.4% in recent trading.

Munich Re Specialty names Prosiegel as Digital Partners head
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Munich Re Specialty–North America has appointed Jascha Prosiegel head of Digital Partners.

Digital Partners provides insurtechs and digital distributors with go-to market partnerships built on Munich Re’s financial strength and expertise.

Prosiegel will lead Digital Partners’ efforts to equip digital brands with tools including capacity, product expertise and data analytics while advancing the team’s goals to bring in new partners.

He succeeds Kate Monahan in the role. Monahan recently moved into a corporate underwriting role within Munich Re Specialty–North America.

Prosiegel joined Munich Re in 2014 as an underwriter for special enterprise risks and has experience in primary and specialty insurance as well as reinsurance.

Most recently, he served as the North American market lead for AI Risks at HSB and Munich Re.

Prosiegel reports to Kevin Johnson, head of North American insurance programs for Munich Re Specialty–North America.

data.title: Citigroup Inc. First Quarter 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Citigroup Inc. has released its financial results for the first quarter of 2025, showcasing a robust performance across its diversified business segments. The company reported a net income of $4.1 billion, or $1.96 per diluted share, on revenues of $21.6 billion. This marks an improvement from the first quarter of 2024, where net income was $3.4 billion, or $1.58 per diluted share, on revenues of $21.0 billion.

Financial Highlights

For the first quarter of 2025, Citigroup's key financial metrics include:

  • Net income of $4.1 billion, up from $3.4 billion in the prior-year period.
  • Earnings per share (EPS) of $1.96, an increase from $1.58 in the first quarter of 2024.
  • Total revenues of $21.6 billion, a 3% increase from $21.0 billion in the prior-year period.
  • Operating expenses decreased by 5% to $13.4 billion.
  • Book value per share increased to $103.90, up 5% from the prior-year period.
  • Tangible book value per share rose to $91.52, a 6% increase from the prior-year period.

Business and Operational Highlights

Citigroup's performance was driven by growth across its five interconnected businesses:

  • Services: Revenues of $4.9 billion, up 3%, with Treasury and Trade Solutions (TTS) revenues increasing by 4%.
  • Markets: Revenues of $6.0 billion, up 12%, driven by growth in both Fixed Income and Equity markets.
  • Banking: Revenues of $2.0 billion, up 12%, with Investment Banking revenues increasing by 12%.
  • Wealth: Revenues of $2.1 billion, up 24%, driven by growth in Citigold, the Private Bank, and Wealth at Work.
  • U.S. Personal Banking (USPB): Revenues of $5.2 billion, up 2%, driven by growth in Branded Cards and Retail Banking.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Citigroup returned approximately $2.8 billion in capital to shareholders, including $1.75 billion in share buybacks. The company also reported a payout ratio of 74% and maintained a Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) Capital ratio of 13.4%.

Management's Perspective

Jane Fraser, Citi CEO, commented on the results, stating, "With net income of $4.1 billion, we delivered a strong quarter, marked by continued momentum, positive operating leverage, and improved returns in each of our five businesses. Services recorded its best first quarter revenue in a decade. Markets had a good first quarter with revenue up 12% driven by strong client activity and monetization."

Future Outlook

Citigroup remains focused on executing its strategy based on a diversified business mix, which is expected to perform well in various macroeconomic scenarios. The company continues to help clients navigate an uncertain environment, leveraging its deep knowledge and breadth of capabilities across global markets.

data.title: Zurich North America recruits CNA’s Sullivan as head of technology
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Zurich North America has added Jeannie Sullivan to its expansive U.S. middle market team with the recruit joining from CNA Insurance as head of technology.

In her new role, Sullivan will lead strategy development and execution for Zurich North America’s technology industry practice.

Having joined Zurich North America, Sullivan reports into Stan Bernard, head of industry practices for U.S. middle market.

Sullivan, who is based in the Minneapolis-St. Paul area, brings 35 years of insurance experience, including in roles focused on the technology industry.

At CNA, Sullivan most recently served as assistant vice president, commercial officer for the carrier’s middle market business unit.

Prior to CNA, Sullivan held leadership positions at Berkley Technology Underwriters, including executive vice president and chief underwriting officer.

Her career also includes stints at OneBeacon Insurance Company, Chubb, The St Paul Companies and PHICO Insurance Company.

Sullivan’s arrival is the second senior appointment Zurich North America has announced in recent weeks, after the carrier earlier this month revealed it had recruited Eric Amadori from Markel as head of management liability, public.

data.description:
Not for release to US wire services or distribution in the United States

QUÉBEC CITY, April 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Robex Resources Inc. (“Robex” or the “Company”) is pleased to announce that it has entered into an underwriting agreement (the “Underwriting Agreement”) with Euroz Hartleys Limited and Canaccord Genuity (Australia) Limited as joint-lead managers (the “Joint Lead Managers”), pursuant to which the Joint Lead Managers will fully underwrite (in their respective proportions) the Company’s proposed offer of CHESS Depositary Interests (each a “CDI”) at an issue price of A$3.11 (CAD$2.73 based the CAD/AUD exchange on April 14, 2025) for gross proceeds of A$120 million (the “Offer”) following closure of a bookbuild for the Offer conducted by the Joint Lead Managers, and the receipt of firm commitments for A$120 million under the bookbuild. Each CDI will represent a beneficial interest in one (1) common share of the Company. The Offer is being conducted in connection with the Company’s proposed additional listing on the Australian Securities Exchange (the “ASX”). The Company intends to conduct the Offer by offering CDIs to purchasers outside of Canada pursuant to a prospectus (the “Prospectus”) to be filed with the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (“ASIC”) and to purchasers in Canada via private placement (the “Private Placement”).

The Offer is subject to certain conditions including, but not limited to, the receipt of all necessary approvals including the approval of the TSX Venture Exchange and all applicable securities regulatory authorities. The Prospectus will not be filed in any province or territory of Canada. The Underwriting Agreement is subject to certain terms and conditions which are customary for an Underwriting Agreement of this type, including conditions precedent, representation and warranties, indemnities (in favour of the JLMs), undertakings and termination rights. In particular, the Underwriting Agreement contains various representation and warranties by the Company relating to the Company and its business.

The JLMs will receive (i) an underwriting fee of 0.25% of the total amount raised under the Offer (“Offer Proceeds”), (ii) a management fee of 1% of the Offer Proceeds and (iii) a distribution fee of 2.75% of the Offer Proceeds, to be paid in cash upon the settlement and issue of CDIs under the Prospectus and the Private Placement. In addition, the Company may, in its absolute discretion, pay the JLMs an incentive fee of 0.5% of the Offer Proceeds.

More information about the Offering will be disclosed on Thursday, April 17, 2025, which is the anticipated date for the Company to file the Prospectus with ASIC in Australia.

About Robex Resources Inc.

Robex is a multi-jurisdictional West African gold production and development company with near-term exploration potential. The Company is dedicated to safe, diverse and responsible operations in the countries in which it operates with a goal to foster sustainable growth. The Company has been operating the Nampala mine in Mali since 2017 and is advancing the Kiniero Gold Project in Guinea.

Robex’s ambition is to become one of the most important mid-tier gold producers in West Africa.

data.title: How to Maintain a Motorcycle for a Great Joy Ride
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 15, 2025 / Owning a motorcycle can bring a sense of freedom and adventure, but keeping it in top shape is essential for a smooth and safe ride.

Regular maintenance not only extends the bike's life but could also help avoid costly repairs. Whether purchased outright or through motorcycle loans, riders should try to ensure their investment lasts.

Here are key maintenance tips to keep a motorcycle running at its best:

Get regular oil changes

Just like a car, a motorcycle's engine relies on clean oil to keep its running smoothly. Oil lubricates moving parts, reduces friction, and helps prevent premature wear and tear. However, over time and use, oil breaks down and collects dirt, making it less effective, increasing the risk of engine damage.

Check the owner's manual to see how often the oil needs changing, but a good rule of thumb is every 2,000 to 10,000 miles, depending on the type of oil used.

Riders who frequently take long trips or ride in extreme weather may need more frequent oil changes. 

Change the air filter

A motorcycle's air filter keeps dirt and debris from entering the engine. A clogged air filter can reduce fuel efficiency and overall performance. Checking and replacing the air filter regularly helps optimize the engine's performance.

Some air filters can be cleaned and reused, while others need to be replaced entirely.

Riders who travel on dusty roads or in high-traffic areas should consider inspecting their air filters more often to prevent buildup.

Inspect parts regularly

A well-maintained motorcycle is generally a safe motorcycle. Riders should routinely inspect key components to ensure everything is working properly, including:

Tires

Check for proper inflation and tread wear. Worn-out tires could lead to dangerous handling issues. Properly inflated tires with good tread improve traction and stability, especially in wet or uneven conditions.

Brakes

Look at brake pads, discs and fluid levels. If braking feels weak, it may be time for a replacement or refill. Squeaky or grinding brakes indicate that the pads need to be replaced to maintain stopping power.

If your brake pads wear down to the metal, they could ruin your brake disc, leading to an expensive repair.

Pro tip: Consider installing braided steel brake lines, which could boost your brake performance by about 50%.

Lighting

Headlights, brake lights and turn signals should all work properly to ensure visibility on the road. Regularly inspect all lights and replace burnt-out bulbs to stay safe, especially when riding at night or in low-visibility conditions.

Exhaust

With the exhaust, watch for rust, leaks, or any unusual sounds that might indicate a problem. Loose fittings or excessive noise could be signs that the exhaust system needs maintenance.

Tune the engine

Tuning your motorcycle generally means making small changes to improve how it runs. This can include minor adjustments, changing fluids, checking valve clearances and doing regular maintenance. These simple tweaks often don't take too long but could make a big difference in your bike's performance and how long it lasts.

Keep the battery in mind

A good battery is a key part that starts a motorcycle and keeps the electrical parts working. A motorcycle battery can last between three to five years on average, depending on use and maintenance. Keeping the battery charged and clean could extend its lifespan.

Check it often for any signs of corrosion, and make sure the connections are clean and secure. If the bike sits for a while, keep the battery charged to extend its life.

Pro tip: If the bike sits unused for long periods, using a battery tender or trickle charger will prevent it from draining completely.

Maintain motorcycle engine coolant

Coolant prevents the engine from overheating, especially in hot weather or long rides. Riders should check coolant levels regularly and top them off if they're low.

Most manufacturers recommend flushing and replacing coolant every two years to maintain efficiency.

Stay safe and enjoy the ride

Proper motorcycle maintenance should ensure a safe and enjoyable ride. Riders should also wear the right protective gear, including a helmet, gloves and appropriate clothing. Following traffic laws and staying alert on the road may also help joy riders avoid accidents.

By taking care of essential maintenance tasks, motorcycle owners could enjoy countless miles of smooth riding. Whether cruising through city streets or feeling the wind on the open road, a well-maintained bike makes all the difference.

15 April 2025

Declaration of First Interim Dividend

Highlights

·    Annualised dividend yield of 6.95%.

·    First quarterly interim dividend of 3.65p per share.

·    Despite volatile market conditions of late, the Board remains confident that the Company's focus on high-quality, dividend-paying companies continues to provide a resilient foundation for delivering positive outcomes over time.

The Board has declared a first quarterly interim dividend of 3.65 pence per share for the year ended 31 December 2025, which will be paid on 23 May 2025 to shareholders on the register at the close of business on 25 April 2025. The ex-dividend date is 24 April 2025.

Based on a share price of 210p on 31 March 2025, this equates to an annualised dividend yield of 6.95%.

This is the first dividend payment announced by the Company's following the introduction of the enhanced dividend policy earlier this year, as part of efforts to broaden the appeal of the Company's shares to a wider range of investors and to reflect the sustained investor appetite for yield in the current interest rate environment.

Despite recent market volatility sparked by US Government announcements on global tariffs, the Board remains confident that the Company's focus on high-quality, dividend-paying businesses provides a resilient foundation for delivering positive long term outcomes. APAC markets, while facing these ongoing geopolitical and trade-related headwinds, continue to offer fertile ground for dividend investing, underpinned by strong corporate balance sheets, robust cash flows, and a growing commitment to shareholder returns.

Asia offers investors some of the most diverse income sources in the world, both geographically and across sectors - from globally competitive technology firms in Taiwan and South Korea, to resource-rich companies in Australia, and high-yielding financial institutions in Singapore and India. This breadth supports a well-balanced income strategy that can weather different market conditions while offering meaningful exposure to long-term growth themes such as urbanisation, rising consumption and digital transformation across the region.

The Company offers the option for shareholders to invest their dividend in a Dividend Reinvestment Plan ('DRIP'), which is managed by the Company's Registrar, Computershare Investor Services PLC. The deadline for elections under the DRIP is 1 May 2025.

Note:

The Company's dividend policy, as announced on 16 January 2025, is to set the dividend at 1.5625% per quarter of the Company's net asset value ("NAV"), equating to approximately 6.25% of NAV per annum. The dividend is calculated using the Company's NAV on the last business day of the preceding financial quarter (i.e. the end of March, June, September and December). The first quarterly interim dividend of 3.65 pence per share is based on 1.5625% of the Company's NAV of 233.63p per share as at 31 March 2025.

data.title: Exclusive: Emerald Bay co-founder and chief risk officer Allkins departs Bain-backed startup
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Emerald Bay co-founder and chief risk officer Miles Allkins has left the company, sources with direct knowledge of the situation said.

The startup specialty (re)insurer was launched in March 2024 asa collaborative underwriting carrier, with more than $100 million in committed capital from Bain Capital.

A spokesperson representing both Emerald Bay and Bain Capital declined to comment. Allkins could not immediately be reached for comment.

Further details on Allkins' departure were not available.

Allkins, who joined Emerald Bay in December 2023, was previously chief underwriting officer at Hippo-owned Spinnaker and held roles at State National, AIR Worldwide and Axis Capital.

The departure comes after Program Manager broke the news in December that CEO Dave Ingrey and head of business development Ken Ingrey, who were also part of the founding team at Spinnaker, were leaving Emerald Bay.

Program Manager was first to report last month that former Liberty Mutual North America programs president Tracey Sharis had been appointed as Emerald Bay's new CEO.

Emerald Bay secured an A-minus VIII financial strength rating at its launch from AM Best as it set about addressing a pipeline of MGA programs opportunities, with a focus on the E&S market.

It was positioned to be able to write admitted and non-admitted business on a nationwide basis and added to its carrier platform by buying Hippo’s Mainsail Insurance Company for $26.6 million.

In November, Emerald Bay struck a deal for its first casualty program, supporting an excess liability facility for risk purchasing group Vanderbilt Properties Associates, which focuses on high-end residential and commercial real estate risks.

The fronting carrier’s first program was unveiled with Arrowhead to support a commercial quake facility. It has also launched a California homeowners facility with Lightspeed Specialty and an E&S commercial property program with Curotech Specialty.

Founding management including Dave and Ken Ingrey were bullish on Emerald Bay's ability to write more than $130 million of gross written premiums in its first year of operation.

It could not immediately be confirmed whether the company has hit that target, although data from S&P Capital IQ shows the carrier had around $40 million in direct written premiums in 2024.

The reasons for the Ingrey exits could not be confirmed, but well-placed sources at the time pointed to a potential divergence of views on the direction of the company.

data.title: Market Chatter: Lucid to Launch Midsize Electric SUV in 2026
data.description:
Lucid Group is on track to enter the midsize electric SUV market in 2026, Reuters reported Tuesday, citing company executives.

The company eyes a price of $50,000 for the model to compete with Ford Motor's (F) Mustang Mach-E, Hyundai Ioniq 5, and Rivian Automotive's (RIVN) upcoming Rivian R2, the report said.

Emad Dlala, senior vice president at Lucid, said the company is readying assembly lines and working with vendors for the planned launch, according to the report.

Lucid's interim Chief Executive Officer Marc Winterhoff told the news outlet that the company is implementing measures to lighten the impact of the Trump administration's tariffs.

On Tuesday, the company said it introduced the 2026 Lucid Gravity Grand Touring, which is priced from $94,900 and can be ordered now.

The company added that the Lucid Gravity Touring, which is planned for later in 2025, starts at $79,900.
